summary:
The term “craters” in this context describes pathological depressions formed by interseptal bone resorption associated with periodontal disease. These lesions can only effectively be addressed via direct structural correction using osseous recontouring surgery, which physically restores lost bony architecture.

Note: This summary assumes an interpretation consistent with standard oral pathology terminology; if not applicable within specific medical domains beyond dentistry/periodontology, further clarification may be needed for accuracy.

Final Answer:

Osseous recontouring
